possibleUpsells: Additional sealer coats for enhanced protection
redFlags: Sealer not included but explicitly recommended ($380 upsell); unsealed concrete will deteriorate faster, especially in Texas weather
Labor costs not itemized separately; unclear breakdown between labor and materials
No drainage or slope grading mentioned; critical for patio longevity to prevent water pooling
No permit mentioned; check local Plano requirements for 800 sqft patio
Curing time mentioned (24-hour for control joints) but no full curing protocol or traffic restrictions specified
No timeline provided for project completion or weather contingencies
data.summary: Quote of $7,800 for 800 sqft broom-finish patio ($9.75/sqft) is fair and well-detailed. Includes proper 4-inch 4000 PSI concrete, #3 rebar reinforcement, compacted gravel base, formwork, and control joints—all essential structural elements. The 5-year warranty is solid. However, sealer is optional ($380 extra) despite being recommended; in Texas heat and humidity, sealing concrete extends life significantly and should likely be included. Labor/material split is not disclosed, making it harder to assess if pricing is competitive. No drainage solution or slope grading mentioned, which is important for a patio's longevity. Overall a competent, transparent quote with good inclusions but gaps on finishing protection and site logistics.

pricingContext.source: ACI 318 / ACI 332, RSMeans residential 2026, NRMCA 2026 ready-mix pricing, BBB concrete complaint patterns, ICRI concrete contractor surveys
